name: Persian (Farsi)
register: Warm, reverent contemporary literary Persian in the tradition of the New Millennium Version (ترجمهٔ هزارهٔ نو, Tarjome-ye Hezāre-ye No, Elam 2003) — the translation most-loved among Iranian believers — with the dignified cadence of the classic Old Persian Version (OPV) behind it. Devotional but not archaic; the intimacy an Iranian Christian would use praying to their own father, not the formality of theological prose.
quote_convention: Guillemets «…» (گیومه) for speech, nested speech in single guillemets ‹…›; RTL punctuation (، ؛ ؟).
poetry_convention: Poetry (Psalms, Prophets, canticles) set as indented broken free-verse lines RTL, parallel/second cola indented one step — as in the NMV Psalter; not classical beyt/mesraʿ couplet layout.

glossary.note: Filial divine name; پدر آسمانی is the natural Persian devotional phrase, + ما for the beloved-child possessive. NOT خدای آسمان ('God of heaven').
Primary filial divine name. پدر (pedar) is warm, personal address a child uses. NEVER revert to خدا (Khodā) or خداوند (Khodāvand).
Persian has no definite article; capitalized/context-marked پدر carries the definite reverent 'the Father'. Kept bare to preserve intimacy, not ذاتِ اقدس-type formality.
Filial divine name without possessive; standard tender form in Iranian Christian prayer and hymnody.
Aramaic address retained (as in the Greek text) + Persian filial gloss پدر; mirrors Rom 8:15 / Mark 14:36 usage familiar from the NMV.
REVERSION TOKEN — generic deity, God-as-God. Universal Persian word (خدا). Use ONLY where English keeps 'God'; never as a substitute for پدرِ ما.

REVERSION TOKEN — traditional Persian rendering of the covenant name (small-caps LORD) in OPV/NMV. Distinct from the generic title 'Lord' for Jesus (خداوند عیسی / سرور). Never a substitute for پدرِ ما.
Fixed standard Persian term (Arabic-loan Rūhu'l-Qudus) in every Persian Bible; by-identity title, do not paraphrase.
Relational construction (روح 'spirit' + پدرِ ما), NOT the by-identity روح‌القدس — matches FHB's relational vision (cf. Gen 1:2 preview).
Standard Persian Gospel title (Pesar-e Ensān).
The Son (Jesus), definite by context; پسر pairs with پدر so the filial Father–Son bond reads plainly.
Christological title; retains خدا (God-as-God) as the fixed confession, not the filial Father. Reviewer to confirm پسرِ خدا vs a filial پسرِ پدر ('Son of the Father').

glossary.gloss: the one and only / unique Son
glossary.note: یگانه (yegāneh, 'sole/unique') is the John 3:16 wording of the NMV — monogenēs, 'one and only'.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially. ملکوت (malakūt) is the reverent biblical 'kingdom' word (vs everyday پادشاهی); + پدرِ ما, never ملکوت خدا.
Dominant Persian Christian name (Īsā), used across OPV/NMV; often عیسی مسیح in full. (یسوع is a rarer variant.)
Standard Persian title (Masīh).
Persian collapses Christ and Messiah to مسیح; where English marks the awaited Hebrew title, موعود ('promised') distinguishes it. Reviewer to confirm vs bare مسیح or transliterated ماشیح.
Standard Persian Bible term (Ahd), as in عهد عتیق/جدید (Old/New Covenant).
Renders Hebrew ḥesed (loyal covenant love). محبت is the biblical agape-word (not romantic عشق); پایدار marks the loyal, unfailing quality. Reviewer to confirm vs محبتِ وفادار ('faithful love').
Renders raḥam / splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy). شفقت is warm, heartfelt pity; رأفت or دلسوزی are alternates. Distinct from bare رحم ('mercy').
Standard Persian theological term (Adālat). Reviewer to confirm vs پارسایی ('uprightness/piety'), which the NMV sometimes uses for personal righteousness.
John 3:16 wording of the NMV (ḥayāt-e jāvedān); زندگی جاویدان is the plainer contemporary alternate.
John 3:3,7 idiom (tavallod-e tāzeh, 'fresh birth'); verbal form از نو متولّد شدن ('to be born anew'). Standard in Persian John 3.
